mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when either series shows month-over-month growth below 0.5 percent, or the interaction term IC remains negative in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equity markets and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes, producing negative 5-day returns when INDPRO rises alongside CPIAUCSL growth exceeding 4.0 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in rolling validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 70 percent of periods following CPIAUCSL spikes above the threshold, or the inflation signal is consistently offset by simultaneous GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly declines in real GDP and monthly increases in the unemployment rate confirm a synchronized economic contraction, which forces equity market repricing and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when GDP declines are isolated to a single quarter without labor market deterioration, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening factory output and inventory drawdowns, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns during periods of inventory restocking, or the predictive accuracy falls below random chance in rolling 1-year validation windows.
